name: Wontons
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat peanut oil in a wok.
2. Sizzle grated ginger and garlic briefly, then add tofu cakes and stir-fry for a few minutes.
3. Add chopped scallions, sesame oil, and tamari soy sauce; stir well until combined.
4. Set aside to cool completely.
5. Prepare your work area with wonton wrappers, lukewarm water, and cornstarch for dusting.
6. Place a wrapper in front of you in a diamond position.
7. Drop a heaping teaspoonful of filling into the center of the wrapper.
8. Moisten all four edges with water.
9. Fold the top corner down to the bottom, folding the wrapper over the filling to form a triangle shape.
10. Press the edges firmly to make a seal.
11. Bring the left and right corners together above the filling.
12. Overlap the tips of these corners, moisten with water, and press together.
13. Place the completed wonton on the cornstarch platter; repeat until all wrappers are used.
14. FOR SOUP: Drop wontons into boiling water and cook for 5 minutes. Drain.
15. TO FRY: Heat 2 to 3 cups of oil in a wok until hot. Deep fry wontons in batches, turning occasionally, until golden brown, about 2 to 3 minutes per side. Remove with tongs and drain on paper towels. Serve with dipping sauce or duck sauce.
Uncooked wontons will keep in the freezer for up to two months if well wrapped. Thaw before frying; they can also be boiled straight from frozen and cooked for an additional 2 minutes.
